assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.29) when 1.28 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states that Regular channel clusters will automatically upgrade to the next available version, with timeline of 1-2 weeks after EoS date

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.28 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response states 12 additional months for Extended channel support, not the correct 24 months total support period

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters face systematic node-level EoS enforcement — nodes on EoS versions are force-upgraded
assertions.reason: Response states No channel clusters will remain on 1.28 indefinitely with no automatic upgrades, missing the critical node-level EoS enforcement behavior

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer EoS force-upgrades for 30 days

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed proactive upgrade recommendations including immediate actions, timeline recommendations, and testing strategies

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging notifications for EoS as a planning tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard for API compatibility checking

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response warns about risks of the No channel cluster including no security patches and unsupported configuration, effectively addressing snowflaking concerns
